Get in touch
004 — Boring Packages

Proprietary tools.
Built on real research.

14 proprietary tools spanning multiomics, molecular docking, FEP simulation, enhanced sampling, AI agents, pharmacovigilance, and clinical reporting. Every package emerged from active research — validated on real data, published or in submission, containerized and version-locked.

01
Unified Multiomics Platform
bsomics v0.4.2 · Proprietary

One pipeline. snRNA-seq, bulk RNA-seq, and spatial transcriptomics — fully integrated.

bsomics is Boring Science's unified cross-modal analysis platform. It processes single-nucleus RNA-seq, bulk RNA-seq, and Visium spatial transcriptomics through a single reproducible workflow — eliminating batch artefacts and manual harmonization between modalities. Built-in modules cover QC, normalization, batch correction, UMAP clustering, cell state annotation, inter-donor variability quantification, pathway module scoring, and spatial deconvolution. Designed for cohorts of any size, from pilot studies to large multi-site datasets.

3
Modalities integrated
36
Max donors profiled
Spot-level
Spatial resolution
✓
Cross-modal coherence
Python snRNA-seq Bulk RNA-seq Spatial Transcriptomics Scanpy / AnnData Cross-Modal
02
Molecular Docking & GNN Scoring
bsdock v1.0.0 · Proprietary

SE(3)-equivariant GNN scoring that outperforms classical docking by 5.5× on binding affinity correlation.

bsdock is Boring Science's computational docking platform, featuring a proprietary SE(3)-equivariant Graph Neural Network scoring function. Protein-ligand complexes are encoded as heterogeneous graphs — separate node types for protein and ligand atoms — with the SE(3)-equivariant architecture guaranteeing predictions are invariant to rotations and translations, a physically grounded constraint that standard empirical scoring functions ignore. bsdock ships a hybrid workflow combining traditional pose generation with GNN rescoring, plus specialist modules for flexible receptor docking, metal coordination, and tethered docking. Compatible with poses from any upstream docking engine.

0.88
PDBbind Pearson R
0.82
Held-out test R
0.94
Activity AUC-ROC
5.5×
vs. best classical scorer
Python PyTorch Geometric SE(3)-Equivariant GNN RDKit Molecular Docking Drug Discovery
03
Physics-Constrained Medical Video Generation
bsvision v0.5.0 · Proprietary

Temporally coherent synthetic medical video — 0.87 SSIM and 12.8% improvement in downstream clinical prediction.

bsvision is Boring Science's medical computer vision package for physics-constrained synthetic video generation. Built on a pixel-space 3D UNet with cross-frame attention at all encoder/decoder levels, it conditions generation on physiological cycle embeddings to enforce temporally consistent cardiac dynamics. Unlike standard video diffusion models that optimize for perceptual quality alone, bsvision enforces physics-based constraints — temporal smoothness, flow consistency, volume conservation, and jerk penalty — producing synthetic videos suitable for clinical AI training data augmentation, simulation, and counterfactual analysis. Validated on echocardiography with a 6.2× improvement in temporal consistency over baseline and a 12.8% gain in downstream ejection fraction prediction accuracy.

0.87
Temporal SSIM
6.2×
Baseline improvement
+12.8%
EF prediction gain
~17.6M
Model parameters
Python PyTorch Video Diffusion Computer Vision Physics-Constrained Medical Imaging
04
Reproducible Genomics Pipelines
bsflow v1.1.0 · Proprietary

GxP-validated Nextflow DSL2 modules — from raw reads to publication-ready output.

bsflow is Boring Science's pipeline infrastructure layer — a library of production-ready Nextflow DSL2 modules for WGS, RNA-seq, ChIP-seq, ATAC-seq, CUT&RUN, and scRNA-seq. Every module ships containerized (Docker and Singularity), with a version-locked dependency manifest and an nf-test suite executed on every commit. The same bsflow pipeline run returns byte-identical output regardless of compute environment — HPC, cloud, or local. Designed from the ground up for reproducibility from research to regulatory submission.

6+
Pipeline types
100%
Reproducibility
Docker + Singularity
Container support
nf-test
Test framework
Nextflow DSL2 nf-core compatible Docker Singularity GxP CI/CD
05
Clinical Genomics Reporting
bsreport v0.3.0 · Proprietary

ACMG-grade variant classification to clinical-ready PDF — in a single command.

bsreport is Boring Science's clinical genomics report generator. It reads VCF input and produces structured HTML and PDF reports with ACMG/AMP variant classification, gnomAD v4 population frequency lookup, ClinVar annotation, and pharmacogenomics (PGx) diplotype calling. Report templates are fully configurable — from internal research summaries to patient-facing clinical documents. Built for diagnostic labs, biobanks, and clinical genomics teams operating under CLIA/CAP frameworks, where report consistency and audit traceability are non-negotiable.

ACMG/AMP
Variant classification
HTML + PDF
Output formats
✓
PGx diplotypes
v4
gnomAD version
Python ACMG/AMP ClinVar gnomAD v4 Pharmacogenomics CLIA/CAP
06
3D Molecular Visualization
bsview v1.0.0 · Proprietary

Web-based 3D molecular visualization with interaction detection, pocket analysis, and 2D interaction maps.

bsview is Boring Science's browser-native molecular visualization platform. It renders protein structures and protein-ligand complexes from PDB input across multiple modes — sticks, spheres, and molecular surfaces — with real-time interaction detection covering hydrogen bonds, hydrophobic contacts, π-stacking, and clash detection. Built-in binding pocket analysis and 2D ligand-protein interaction maps make it a complete structural analysis environment. No installation required — runs entirely in-browser via WebGL, deployable as a standalone app or embedded in any data platform.

3
Rendering modes
5+
Interaction types detected
Browser / Embed
Deployment
PDB / mmCIF
Input format
React Three.js / WebGL Molecular Visualization Protein-Ligand Browser-native
07
AI-Powered Bioinformatics Agent
bsagent v0.2.0 · Proprietary

Conversational AI agent for automated drug discovery and NGS analysis — 17 built-in bioinformatics tools.

bsagent is Boring Science's autonomous bioinformatics AI agent. Powered by a Claude-based ReAct architecture, it executes multi-step drug discovery and next-generation sequencing analysis workflows through a natural language interface. It ships with 17 integrated bioinformatics tools — covering virtual screening, differential expression, variant calling, pathway analysis, and 3D molecule visualization — accessible via CLI, REST API, or web interface. Jobs run asynchronously and are audit-logged, making bsagent suitable for both exploratory analysis and production pipeline orchestration.

17
Built-in tools
CLI · API · Web
Interfaces
Claude
LLM backbone
✓
Async execution
Python FastAPI Claude API ReAct Agent Drug Discovery NGS
08
Molecular Dynamics Analysis
bsmd v0.3.0 · Proprietary

End-to-end MD trajectory analysis — RMSD, RMSF, hydrogen bonding, clustering, and publication-quality output.

bsmd is Boring Science's molecular dynamics analysis suite. It processes trajectory output from GROMACS, AMBER, NAMD, and OpenMM and produces a structured, reproducible analysis report covering RMSD/RMSF, radius of gyration, secondary structure evolution, hydrogen bond occupancy, hydrophobic contact persistence, binding pocket stability, and conformational clustering. Every analysis run is containerized and version-locked — making MD post-processing as reproducible as the simulation itself.

4
MD engines supported
10+
Analysis modules
CSV · PDF · HTML
Output formats
✓
Reproducible
Python MDAnalysis GROMACS / AMBER Trajectory Analysis Clustering
09
Pharmacovigilance & Drug Safety Intelligence
bspv v0.4.0 · Proprietary

Automated pharmacovigilance signal detection — FAERS integration, disproportionality analysis, and regulatory-grade reporting.

bspv is Boring Science's pharmacovigilance intelligence platform. It ingests FDA Adverse Event Reporting System (FAERS) data, structured safety databases, and literature-derived signals to run automated disproportionality analyses (PRR, ROR, BCPNN). Integrated with genomic variant data, bspv links ADR signals to patient-specific pharmacogenomic backgrounds — enabling mechanistic safety hypotheses before compounds enter clinical trials. Outputs are formatted to ICH E2B standards for regulatory submission.

3 (PRR · ROR · BCPNN)
Signal detection methods
✓
FAERS integration
✓
PGx linkage
ICH E2B
Output standard
Python FAERS Disproportionality Analysis Pharmacovigilance Drug Safety ICH E2B
10
R-Group Design & Medicinal Chemistry
bsrgroup v0.2.0 · Proprietary

Enumerate, score, and rank R-group substitutions for lead optimization — scaffold-in, ranked library out.

bsrgroup is Boring Science's R-group designer for structure-activity relationship exploration. It takes a scaffold SMILES and a set of R-group attachment points, enumerates substitution libraries, and scores each candidate against predicted ADMET properties, synthetic accessibility, and docking affinity via bsdock integration. The output is a ranked compound library with per-property scores and a 2D SAR heatmap — ready for experimental prioritization or downstream virtual screening.

Automated
Enumeration
✓
bsdock integration
✓
ADMET scoring
2D heatmap
SAR visualization
Python RDKit SMILES Medicinal Chemistry Lead Optimization SAR
11
Chemical Property Analysis
bschem v0.3.0 · Proprietary

Batch chemical property calculation — Lipinski, TPSA, logP, toxicity flags, and structural alerts at scale.

bschem is Boring Science's chemical analyzer — a batch processing engine for computing physicochemical and ADMET properties across compound libraries of any size. It calculates Lipinski Ro5, Veber, and REOS filters alongside TPSA, logP, logD, pKa, aqueous solubility, metabolic stability predictions, and structural alert flags including PAINS and reactive groups. Designed for hit-to-lead triage and compound library health assessment, with CSV, SDF, and interactive HTML output.

20+
Properties calculated
Ro5 · Veber · REOS
Filter sets
✓
PAINS alerts
CSV · SDF · HTML
Output
Python RDKit ADMET PAINS Filters Physicochemical Library Analysis
12
Free Energy Perturbation Simulation
bsfep v0.1.0 · Beta

Alchemical FEP and thermodynamic integration for rigorous binding free energy prediction at lead optimization scale.

bsfep is Boring Science's free energy perturbation platform. It automates relative binding free energy (RBFE) and absolute binding free energy (ABFE) calculations using alchemical perturbation theory, with support for GROMACS and OpenMM backends. The workflow handles lambda window setup, Bennett Acceptance Ratio (BAR) and MBAR analysis, cycle closure correction, and uncertainty quantification. Designed for lead series where rank-ordering by docking score is insufficient — bsfep delivers thermodynamically rigorous ΔΔG predictions targeting sub-kcal/mol accuracy on benchmarked systems.

RBFE + ABFE
FEP type
BAR · MBAR
Free energy estimators
GROMACS · OpenMM
MD backends
<1 kcal/mol
Target accuracy
Python Alchemical FEP GROMACS OpenMM MBAR / BAR Lead Optimization
13
Constant-pH Molecular Dynamics
bsphmd v0.1.0 · Beta

pH-aware MD with continuous protonation state sampling — resolve pH-dependent binding and active site dynamics.

bsphmd is Boring Science's constant-pH molecular dynamics package. It enables explicit protonation state sampling during simulation using continuous constant-pH MD (CpHMD), capturing pH-dependent conformational dynamics that fixed-protonation simulations miss entirely. Titratable residues — His, Asp, Glu, Cys, Lys — are sampled at physiological or user-specified pH, with free energy profiles reporting per-residue pKa shifts in the binding context. Critical for targets with ionizable active sites, allosteric pH sensors, or compounds with titratable functional groups.

CpHMD
Method
His · Asp · Glu · Cys · Lys
Titratable residues
✓
pKa output
Configurable
pH range
Python Constant-pH MD AMBER Protonation States pKa Prediction pH-Dependent Binding
14
REMD & Enhanced Sampling
bsremd v0.1.0 · Beta

Replica Exchange MD and simulated annealing for conformational space exploration — escape local minima systematically.

bsremd is Boring Science's enhanced sampling simulation package. It orchestrates Replica Exchange Molecular Dynamics (T-REMD) across temperature ladders and Hamiltonian REMD (H-REMD) across alchemical states, enabling efficient conformational space exploration for flexible proteins, intrinsically disordered regions, and peptides. Built-in simulated annealing protocols accelerate structure optimization and docking pose refinement. Automated exchange acceptance monitoring, replica overlap diagnostics, and Weighted Histogram Analysis (WHAM) for free energy surface reconstruction are included. Compatible with GROMACS, AMBER, and NAMD.

T-REMD · H-REMD · SA
Sampling methods
WHAM
Free energy analysis
GROMACS · AMBER · NAMD
MD backends
✓
Exchange diagnostics
Python REMD Simulated Annealing Enhanced Sampling GROMACS / AMBER WHAM

Interested in running our pipelines on your data? We partner with research groups, pharma, and diagnostics labs for pilot engagements.

On reproducibility

"Every package ships with a test suite, a lockfile, and a container. If it works on our machine, it works on yours — and on the FDA's."

— Boring Science Build Standard
